comment.body:
As you know, Alma made it clear she doesn’t see a directional edge on either side, which makes sense. Most funds are doing ultra-fast mean-reversion trading, or are positioned in Europe and in the vol market. That’s why Alma chose a short-vol strategy, and it’s a very smart call. Obviously, if you can’t short the VIX or vega, you can’t follow that trade. In that case your best bet is just to sit on your hands. You won’t starve missing a week or two...
We can all see Trump charging ahead, bending the knee in the Middle East, and the market is being propped up, but there are no real results. This rally is driven purely by short squeezes and vol-selling triggering a vanna squeeze, exactly as Our Lady described. Until there’s real action, there’s no green light. What you’re seeing now is all algo flow. You don’t even know how many firms and funds are betting on 5k being hit. So Alma isn’t talking out of her ass,watch your fomo..

comment.body: A lot of people are impatient, but right now, patience is the single best play. Many of you want a straight up or down call. Today you got two signals, and both plays netted nice profits. But if you don’t understand why you’re doing the trade, you’ll still blow your gains, no matter how right the signal was. This morning Alma asked what you took away from her post and how you’re planning to act at those levels. How will you make your decisions? If you can’t grasp the rationale behind the signal, you’ll bail too early, then fade back in only after it’s too late…

comment.body:
one Q here.
I generally agree there's all this timing to support the market. That's my thesis as well.  But we've also heard about a "controlled walk down" so to speak, and this rally has FAR exceeded that. 10 straight up days, constant substance-less pumps, etc.  So I'm currently trying to figure out the "why" at these levels if the goal is down. And that's a good goal because down gets the administration their cuts. I bet if market was $5,200 Powell would have been FAR more dovish yesterday.

comment.body: Yeah, but it ain’t Alma’s fault. People here keep acting like rookie retail traders. These have been wild times, you can’t even imagine the chaos the funds have been through, and it isn’t over until we see real progress, like Alma keeps stressing. And don’t forget, she was the first I saw calling the “hidden QE” before opex, and she nailed it, way before Nomura or Goldman’s inside reports, while still warning about the very real risks. You’re just salty ’cause your FOMO’s kicking in. Plus, today she pointed out that Trump’s announcement was timed to halt a pullback, and that’s exactly what happened. Folks really need to learn how to interpret texts. You’ve all been handed tons of top-tier analysis

comment.body: This is exactly what I wrote up above you, that you don’t understand the trade signal. Alma isn’t shorting the ES mini because doomsday is tomorrow. She gave us a clear, high-probability signal and said she sees 5720 as the top, which makes perfect sense based on the gamma chart. The market then pulled back 28 points, and since the puts were underpriced, they printed nicely. That’s a straightforward reversion trade. And in my read, using 1DTE options means two things. One, to dodge theta decay, since she wasn’t expecting a sharp reversal in a rangebound market that suppresses vol..exactly what she’s betting on. And two, if the move gains confirmation, this could mark the start of a slightly larger pullback within the range, especially since the UK didn’t buy the deal narrative. So please, don’t be so touchy, there’s no need to take everything as a personal attack

comment.body: it is all math, bcs at very high sigma, the log-normal distribution of St is so broad that a tiny move in S or a little but of extra theta or even a twak in vega barely shifts the probability mass relative to an almost sure exercise
